comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around appreciation and technical interest in the dithering technique used in the game Return of the Obra Dinn. Several users share personal connections to the art style, references to related videos and devlogs, and mixed opinions on the gameplay experience.

comments.author: m12k
comments.text: If you find this interesting, you might also be interested in this video of someone diving even deeper into how to make the dither surface stable: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HPqGaIMVuLs
comments.analysis: Provides a relevant resource that deepens understanding of the dithering technique, adding value to the discussion.

comments.author: paultendo
comments.text: What a fascinating deep dive. 2x with sphere mapping is my favourite - it starts to take on a sort of pointillism-like quality which gives all the objects (or maybe my brain) a sort of understanding of their texture.
comments.analysis: Offers a thoughtful interpretation of the dithering effect, connecting it to an artistic style which enriches the technical discussion.

comments.author: martinknopf
comments.text: I remember following dukope‘s well-written devlog back then. Even tried to reproduce his edge detection for a game jam. Thanks for digging this out.
comments.analysis: Shares personal experience related to the technical content, indicating familiarity and constructive engagement.

comments.author: progforlyfe
comments.text: I grew up on the small 6 inch 1 bit Mac SE display so the art style has a special place in my heart. Sadly I'm too "dumb" to fully enjoy the game as it requires a lot of attention to detail -- amazing if you enjoy detective style puzzles! I still highly respect it.
comments.analysis: Provides a personal and emotional perspective on the art style, adding depth to the discussion though less technical.

comments.author: surfsvammel
comments.text:
I absolutely loved this game. I still think about it often, the story and the characters. I wish there was more games like it.

Interesting read!
comments.analysis: Expresses genuine appreciation for the game and its narrative, contributing a positive subjective viewpoint.

comments.author: rokkamokka
comments.text: I gave this game a shot but honestly the art style got in the way of the gameplay for me. Fun to read how much effort went into it
comments.analysis: Offers a critical personal opinion on the game's art style affecting gameplay, balancing the discussion with a less favorable view.

comments.author: jay_sar
comments.text: Actually saw a great youtube video about this recently - very cool how they were able to accomplish this. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_3qzyAHMoUU
comments.analysis: Mentions a relevant external video resource, though without detailed commentary, providing moderate value.

comments.author: HelloUsername
comments.text: Previous discussion of "Stabilizing the Obra Dinn 1-bit dithering process (2017)" on 08-nov-2024 https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=42084080 114 comments
comments.analysis: References a prior discussion but adds little new insight or analysis, making it less useful.
